summary: Adjusted gmadventure section metadata and symbol declarations so its data, bss, sbss, and sdata2 boundaries/scopes better reflect the actual objects. The PR also moved the gm_803DE650_t struct definition from gmadventure.c into gm/types.h and exported gmadventure data arrays through gmadventure.h, making shared data declarations consistent with the corrected symbol map.
what_changed: Changed the gmadventure sbss split in config/GALE01/splits.txt from end 0x804D68E8 to end 0x804D68E4.
Corrected gm_80490910 in symbols.txt from size 0x30 global byte data to size 0x2E local byte data.
Corrected gm_804D68D8 and gm_804D68E0 sbss symbols from global 0x8-sized objects to local objects with sizes 0x7 and 0x4 respectively.

Marked several gmadventure sdata2 constants at 0x804DAC18 through 0x804DAC2C as local instead of global and merged the small split entries at 0x804DAC24/0x804DAC26 and 0x804DAC28/0x804DAC2A into 0x3-byte local objects.
Removed the local static definition of struct gm_803DE650_t from gmadventure.c and replaced it with use of struct gm_803DE650_t from a header.
Changed gmadventure.h to include melee/gm/types.h instead of melee/gm/forward.h so it can expose typed data declarations.
Added extern declarations in gmadventure.h for gm_803DE1B8_MinorScenes[49] and gm_803DE650[46].
smart_moves: Fixed section boundaries and symbol metadata together, avoiding a mismatch where splits.txt and symbols.txt would describe different data extents.
Moved gm_803DE650_t into the shared gm types header before exporting gm_803DE650, allowing the extern declaration to use the real struct type instead of an incomplete or duplicated local definition.
Converted data symbols that are evidently file-local implementation details from global scope to local scope in symbols.txt, which better matches how the data is declared in C.
Preserved the existing anonymous-address naming style for unknown data symbols while improving sizes and scopes, minimizing churn while correcting layout metadata.
decomp_lessons: When fixing data sections, update both config/GALE01/splits.txt and config/GALE01/symbols.txt so split extents, object sizes, and local/global scopes remain synchronized.
If a data object needs to be declared in a public module header, move its struct definition out of the C file into an appropriate shared types header instead of duplicating the type.
Small adjacent sdata2 byte/halfword symbols may actually be one local aggregate object; verify object sizes rather than preserving overly granular auto-generated symbols.
Correct local/global symbol scope can matter for matching and for preventing headers from exposing implementation-only constants unnecessarily.
Use array lengths in extern declarations when the size is known from the defining data table; this documents the data layout and helps future type checking.
naming_conventions: Unknown gmadventure data symbols retain address-based names such as gm_80490910, gm_804D68D8, and gm_804DAC18.
Scene table naming uses the pattern gm_803DE1B8_MinorScenes for a MinorScene array anchored at its address.
The table struct is named struct gm_803DE650_t, combining the address-based data name with a _t suffix for the associated type.
Function declarations in gmadventure.h retain address comments and descriptive suffixes such as gm_801B51CC_OnInit and gm_801B5214_OnLoad.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Tightened bss/sbss object sizes and section endpoints to match the actual emitted layout, including shrinking the gmadventure sbss split end by 4 bytes.
Changed symbol scope annotations to local where the C definition is file-local or where constants should not be exported.
Represented sdata2 constants as correctly sized local objects instead of multiple misleading global byte/halfword symbols.
Centralized the gm_803DE650_t layout in a header so the data table can be typed consistently without changing the table initializer.
follow_up_queries: Check later gmadventure PRs for whether gm_803DE650_t fields x0 through xF were renamed or documented.
Search for other gm module data sections where splits.txt sbss endpoints disagree with symbols.txt object sizes.
Audit address-based sdata2 symbols near gm_804DAC18 for possible typed constants or aggregate structures.
